title: PM Modi’s gifts to French, Slovak leaders showcase India’s cultural heritage

Slovak officials were honored with Kashmiri carpets, Dokra art, Himroo textiles, and ancient Ayurvedic texts, showcasing India's rich craftsmanship and intellectual legacy.

These claims emerged during a declassification review, with whistleblowers alleging retaliation.

The challenge comes after a recent ad featuring Harbhajan reportedly reopened old wounds for Sreesanth.

Srikkanth urged patience, highlighting the 15-year-old's dominant style and unique aura, stating he is "simply outstanding for Indian cricket" and "the son of God" in cricketing terms.

title: Tamil Nadu passes resolution against Karnataka’s Mekedatu dam project
description: Tamil Nadu's Assembly unanimously passed a resolution urging the Union government to deny all clearances for Karnataka's Mekedatu dam project. The resolution cited violations of tribunal and Supreme Court orders, emphasizing the Cauvery basin's deficit status and the need for concurrence from basin states and the Union government before any new water storage projects are undertaken.

title: 'Haven't learnt that lesson yet': Trump denies limits to his power after Iran war
description: President Trump declared no limits to U.S. power after a peace agreement with Iran, digitally signed to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Despite critics noting a retreat from initial demands, Trump insisted the deal represented a military victory and demonstrated American strength, not limitations.

title: Watch: India’s first LNG carrier reaches Gujarat as Hormuz shipping resumes
description: The LNG carrier Disha has successfully arrived at Dahej Port in Gujarat, heralding a much-anticipated return of maritime activities through the Strait of Hormuz amidst recent tensions in the West Asia region. This docking comes on the heels of a historic US-Iran peace accord, suggesting a potential stabilization of India's vital energy supply lines.

Wife can't be forced into narco test: HC rejects plea seeking tests to rebut allegation
description: Rajasthan High Court dismissed a husband's late plea for narco-analysis, polygraph, and DNA tests in a divorce case. The court ruled the application was filed too late, after evidence closed, and was unnecessary to decide the wife's allegations of sexual incapacity. Spouses cannot be forced into such tests without consent.

description: Uttar Pradesh minister Om Prakash Rajbhar launched a sharp attack on Samajwadi Party leader Ram Gopal Yadav, accusing him of casteist remarks and predicting a party split. Rajbhar claimed Yadav viewed Rajbhar and Maurya communities as inferior to Yadavs.

India's world-class airports and the first-impression challenge facing its cities
description: Many Indian cities struggle to impress travellers from the air, often revealing informal settlements and pollution, unlike the orderly, greener landscapes of Singapore, Bangkok, or Colombo. This stark contrast stems from rapid urbanisation, immense population density, and governance challenges, despite India's economic growth. While infrastructure is improving, the sheer scale of the problem presents a significant hurdle.

Delhi HC upholds Centre's Telegram ban order ahead of NEET retest
description: Delhi High Court upheld the Centre's temporary ban on Telegram, citing its potential misuse for exam fraud and misinformation ahead of the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination. The government's order, issued under the IT Act, was deemed well-founded. The restriction aims to prevent cheating rackets from exploiting the platform, with the ban in effect until June 22.

Psychiatrist explains why you pick at skin and nails during conversations
description: Unconscious nail biting and skin picking often stem from stress and anxiety, acting as ingrained coping mechanisms. Experts explain these automatic behaviors are triggered by the brain seeking comfort or relief. Self-awareness techniques and keeping hands busy can help, but persistent physical harm or distress necessitates professional intervention for underlying causes.

Leaders from Kerala also highlighted his journey and outreach efforts.

In days before he killed house help, friends saw marked change in doctor
description: South Delhi residents are baffled by the arrest of dermatologist Manish Gupta for allegedly murdering his domestic help. While neighbours saw him as reserved, friends noted behavioral changes and recalled he may have been treated for depression. Gupta was known for his charitable work and professional achievements, making the accusation of violence against a long-time employee difficult to comprehend.

Man thrashes wife, tonsures her head; arrested after video surfaces
description: A shocking incident unfolded in Korea district, Chhattisgarh, where a man was arrested for brutally assaulting his wife. He allegedly tonsured her head and forced her to drink urine. The FIR was registered after disturbing videos surfaced online. The woman, who had a love marriage 15 years ago, accused her husband of character assassination and abandonment prior to the attack.

title: When is Father's Day? History, significance, origin story, and all you need to know
description: Father's Day, celebrated on the third Sunday of June, honors the vital roles fathers play. Originating in the early 20th century with Sonora Smart Dodd, the day officially became a U.S. observance in 1972. It's a time to express gratitude for their love, sacrifices, and guidance, acknowledging their evolving contributions to families and society.
